_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Severe stiffness of the knee joint
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 10 years of experience
clinical_indications: Failure of conservative treatment for knee stiffness
Severe stiffness of the knee joint
Pain and decreased ROM in stiff TKR

common_queries: Clinical findings and X-ray report not indicative of surgery
Clinical notes with indication for surgery not submitted
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part not indicative of surgery
Clinical photograph of affected part not submitted or unclear
Detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details not provided
Post-procedure X-ray (labelled with patient ID, date and side) of affected part not submitted
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes not available
Discharge summary with follow-up advice not provided at discharge

clinical_key_pointers: Open arthrolysis is indicated after failure of conservative treatment and in cases of severe stiffness of the knee joint.

A meticulous open arthrolysis, with consideration of selective patellar resurfacing and/or tibial-insert downsizing, is a valid treatment strategy for this difficult and poorly understood problem of pain and decreased ROM. It gives a significant and sustainable improvement in range of movement.
Open arthrolysis is an effective and reproducible technique in the treatment of the stiff TKR and has good, long-term results.
